target.title: [Fix] Deliver QQBot partial tool progress
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: QQBot buffered text-only `kind: "tool"` deliveries and discarded them when a later final block arrived.
- Solution: deliver visible text-only tool progress immediately when QQBot streaming progress is enabled.
- What changed: kept the fix in QQBot outbound delivery, added focused regression coverage, and documented the fix in the changelog.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): shared reply semantics, media fallback, final block delivery, and official C2C streaming ownership stay unchanged.

## Motivation

Users with partial streaming configured should see the assistant's pre-tool acknowledgment instead of silence until the final block reply arrives. Closes #66509.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #66509
- Related N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: QQBot text-only `kind: "tool"` progress no longer disappears when a later `kind: "block"` response arrives under partial streaming.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout running the QQBot outbound dispatcher path on macOS.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Invoked `dispatchOutbound` with a C2C inbound message, `streaming: { mode: "partial" }`, a text-only tool delivery, then a final block delivery; repeated with `streaming: false`.
Evidence after fix: Terminal transcript from the local dispatcher run:

```text
partial streaming enabled:
sendText -> Working: checking logs
sendText -> final answer

streaming off:
sendText -> final answer
```

Observed result after fix: The configured partial-streaming path emits the tool progress before the final reply; the off path preserves the old final-only behavior.
What was not tested: Live QQBot network transport and official C2C streaming transport.
Before evidence: Before the patch, the same dispatcher sequence only produced `sendText -> final answer` for the partial-streaming case.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.ts` stored text-only `kind: "tool"` payloads in `toolTexts` for the tool-only fallback, then cleared the fallback timer when a later block response arrived without ever flushing that text.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no QQBot dispatcher regression test covered tool progress followed by a final block under partial streaming.
- Contributing context (if known): media fallback and final block delivery shared the same tool buffer path, so text-only progress had no immediate-delivery lane.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: text-only tool progress is sent immediately in partial streaming mode and remains buffered when streaming is off.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the QQBot owner state machine that buffered and discarded the payload.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

QQBot accounts with partial streaming progress enabled now send text-only tool progress before the final answer instead of silently dropping it.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
user message -> tool text buffered -> final block arrives -> buffered text discarded

After:
user message -> text-only tool progress delivered -> final block delivered
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS local checkout
- Runtime/container: Node via `scripts/run-vitest.mjs`
- Model/provider: captured dispatcher callback
- Integration/channel (if any): QQBot outbound dispatcher
- Relevant config (redacted): `streaming: { mode: "partial" }` and `streaming: false`

### Steps

1. Emit a text-only `kind: "tool"` payload.
2. Emit a later `kind: "block"` payload.
3. Compare sent QQBot text calls with partial streaming enabled and disabled.

### Expected

- Partial streaming sends tool progress, then final answer.
- Streaming off sends only the final answer.

### Actual

- Matches expected after this patch.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: partial streaming sends text-only tool progress immediately; streaming off keeps old buffered behavior.
- Edge cases checked: media and voice payloads do not use the immediate text-only path; official C2C streaming is excluded from this static progress path.
- What you did **not** verify: live QQBot network delivery.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: tool progress could duplicate official C2C streaming output.
  - Mitigation: official C2C streaming keeps ownership; this immediate static path is disabled when official C2C streaming is active.
target.author: samzong
